title: Record Your First Sample
description: Learn the basics of sampling audio into the PO-33 using the built-in microphone or line input.

steps.title: Choose Your Sound Slot
steps.instruction: Turn the sound knob to select the slot where you want to record your sample. Slots 1-8 are for melodic sounds (can be played at different pitches), slots 9-16 are for drums (fixed pitch).
steps.tip: Start with slot 9 for your first drum sample, or slot 1 for a melodic sound.

steps.title: Prepare Your Audio Source
steps.instruction: Decide whether to use the built-in microphone or connect an external audio source via the line input jack.

substeps.instruction: For microphone: Position sound source 6-12 inches away
For line input: Connect a 3.5mm cable from your audio source

steps.tip: The line input gives cleaner recordings. The mic adds lo-fi character.

steps.title: Enter Sampling Mode
steps.instruction: Hold the record button. The display will show the input level meter - you'll see activity when sound is detected.
steps.tip: Keep holding record throughout the sampling process.

steps.title: Record the Sample
steps.instruction: While still holding record, press any pad to start recording. Play your audio source. Press the same pad again to stop recording.

substeps.instruction: Press the same pad to stop

steps.warning: Recording to an existing sound slot will replace its contents!

steps.title: Play Your Sample
steps.instruction: Release the record button to exit sample mode. Now press the pad corresponding to your selected sound slot to hear your sample play back.
steps.tip: If it's a melodic sound (1-8), hold the pad and press other pads to change pitch!

outcomes: Recorded your first sample into the PO-33
Understanding of melodic vs drum sound slots
Ability to choose between microphone and line input
